window.label: Entire history
window.phrase: the entire available history up to 2026-07-22

evidence.claim: On July 29, 2024, Masdar advanced terms for a power purchase agreement for the 500MW Gad solar project in Ethiopia.
evidence.snippet: Masdar and Ethiopia's Ministry of Innovation advanced terms for the development of the 500-megawatt Gad solar project.

evidence.claim: DP World and the Ethiopian Shipping and Logistics Services signed a transit agreement on September 2, 2024, to utilize the Berbera Corridor.
evidence.snippet: The transit agreement streamlines custom clearance procedures for Ethiopian import and export cargo passing through Berbera Port.

evidence.claim: The Abu Dhabi Fund for Development (ADFD) signed a USD 100 million agreement on October 18, 2024, to support Ethiopia's financial reforms.
evidence.snippet: ADFD agreed to provide a USD 100 million budget support package to facilitate Ethiopia's economic stabilization reforms.

evidence.claim: UAE's EDGE Group signed a joint venture with Ethiopia's Defense Industrial Sector on November 15, 2024, to establish a drone assembly line.
evidence.snippet: EDGE Group partnered with Ethiopian defense manufacturers to establish a local assembly line for tactical unmanned systems.

evidence.claim: G42 signed a memorandum of understanding with Ethiopia's Artificial Intelligence Institute on January 10, 2025, for regional language processing.
evidence.snippet: G42 partnered with Ethiopia's AI Institute to develop specialized large language models for East African local languages.

evidence.claim: Ethiopian Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed met with UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed in Abu Dhabi on February 22, 2025, discussing trade ties.
evidence.snippet: The leaders reviewed bilateral relations, highlighting investments in green energy, high-tech agricultural farming, and regional security.

evidence.claim: AD Ports Group signed a strategic agreement with Ethiopia on March 15, 2025, to develop dry port logistics facilities near Modjo.
evidence.snippet: AD Ports Group agreed to invest in modernizing key Ethiopian inland terminals to improve regional trade supply chains.

evidence.claim: UAE-based agricultural firm Al Dahra announced a USD 40 million investment in commercial forage farming in Ethiopia on April 28, 2025.
evidence.snippet: Al Dahra finalized plans to develop large-scale commercial farming ventures in Ethiopia to secure agricultural exports to the GCC.

evidence.claim: flydubai increased its flight frequency between Dubai and Addis Ababa to double-daily flights starting June 1, 2025.
evidence.snippet: flydubai doubled its flights to Addis Ababa to handle rising freight demand for Ethiopian horticultural exports.

evidence.claim: The UAE and Ethiopia signed a comprehensive double taxation avoidance agreement on July 15, 2025, to promote reciprocal investments.
evidence.snippet: The bilateral treaty was signed to remove investment barriers and prevent double taxation for companies operating in both nations.

edges.claim: On July 29, 2024, Masdar advanced terms for a power purchase agreement for the 500MW Gad solar project in Ethiopia.
DP World and the Ethiopian Shipping and Logistics Services signed a transit agreement on September 2, 2024, to utilize the Berbera Corridor.
The Abu Dhabi Fund for Development (ADFD) signed a USD 100 million agreement on October 18, 2024, to support Ethiopia's financial reforms.
UAE's EDGE Group signed a joint venture with Ethiopia's Defense Industrial Sector on November 15, 2024, to establish a drone assembly line.
G42 signed a memorandum of understanding with Ethiopia's Artificial Intelligence Institute on January 10, 2025, for regional language processing.
Ethiopian Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed met with UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed in Abu Dhabi on February 22, 2025, discussing trade ties.
AD Ports Group signed a strategic agreement with Ethiopia on March 15, 2025, to develop dry port logistics facilities near Modjo.
flydubai increased its flight frequency between Dubai and Addis Ababa to double-daily flights starting June 1, 2025.
The UAE and Ethiopia signed a comprehensive double taxation avoidance agreement on July 15, 2025, to promote reciprocal investments.
Co-mentioned in the same evidence record (E2) — plausible unstated relationship.
